frozen_components: latent hidden-state representation (shaped by curriculum, not directly by RL)
architecture.summary: SWITCH is a switchable latent reasoning framework that augments a Qwen3-8B base model with explicit boundary tokens <swi> and </swi> to demarcate hidden-state recurrence blocks. Information flows as follows: during inference, the model emits <swi> to enter latent mode, runs one or more recurrent <latent> steps (each feeding the previous step's last-layer hidden state as the next input embedding, following Coconut-style recurrence), then emits </swi> to exit and resume text decoding. Training comprises three phases: Phase 1 SFT marks high-entropy reasoning spans with boundaries; Phase 2 curriculum progressively replaces visible text inside boundaries with <latent> positions while preserving boundary signals; Phase 3 Switch-GRPO applies on-policy RL via Group Relative Policy Optimization, with gradients flowing through text positions only and latent positions contributing deterministically via frozen KV cache. The boundary tokens make the policy ratio well-defined at every text position and serve as anchors for mechanistic probing and causal intervention.

biblio.title: Demystifying Hidden-State Recurrence: Switchable Latent Reasoning with On-Policy Reinforcement Learning

claims.statement: Switch reaches 79.3% accuracy on MATH-500 and 89.2% on GSM8K, above all Coconut-style baselines under the matched-base-model protocol
Switch-GRPO over the SFT-only checkpoint raises latent-conditional accuracy by 12.6 points while halving the latent invocation rate from 81% to 58%
<swi> achieves a probability of 0.203 and rank 1 at annotated switch positions vs. 0.068 probability and rank 1003.9 at random non-boundary positions (After SFT checkpoint)
Linear probe accuracy for 'next token is <swi>' at the last layer is 0.919 (After SFT) and 0.884 (After Switch-GRPO)

claims.dataset: MATH-500 diagnostic subset (problems with correct latent reasoning)
claims.statement: Zeroing the latent hidden state in causal intervention collapses diagnostic-subset accuracy from 1.000 to 0.333, while a same-norm random vector costs only 0.095 points and skipping the latent step costs 0.190 points

Experiments are restricted to 8B-parameter Qwen3 models and to mathematical reasoning benchmarks (MATH-500 and GSM8K); Switch has not been evaluated on multi-domain reasoning or at larger model scales

Switch-GRPO's gradient flows through text segments only; latent positions contribute via frozen KV cache, so the latent representation is shaped primarily by Phase 2 curriculum rather than directly by the RL objective

The latent block computation reduces to a single hidden-state transition on entry, kept from collapsing by the minimum-dwell constraint, while the curriculum's fixed-offset </swi> makes the remainder exit-ready
